verses.latin_vulgate: Ne glorieris in crastinum,  ignorans quid superventura pariat dies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַֽל־תִּ֭תְהַלֵּל בְּי֣וֹם מָחָ֑ר כִּ֤י לֹֽא־תֵ֝דַ֗ע מַה־יֵּ֥לֶד יֽוֹם׃
verses.king_james: Boast not thyself of to morrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Laudet te alienus, et non os tuum ;  extraneus, et non labia tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהַלֶּלְךָ֣ זָ֣ר וְלֹא־פִ֑יךָ נׇ֝כְרִ֗י וְאַל־שְׂפָתֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Let another man praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips.

verses.latin_vulgate: Grave est saxum, et onerosa arena,  sed ira stulti utroque gravior.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּֽבֶד־אֶ֭בֶן וְנֵ֣טֶל הַח֑וֹל וְכַ֥עַס אֱ֝וִ֗יל כָּבֵ֥ד מִשְּׁנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: A stone is heavy, and the sand weighty; but a fool’s wrath is heavier than them both.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ira non habet misericordiam nec erumpens furor,  et impetum concitati ferre quis poterit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַכְזְרִיּ֣וּת חֵ֭מָה וְשֶׁ֣טֶף אָ֑ף וּמִ֥י יַ֝עֲמֹ֗ד לִפְנֵ֥י קִנְאָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before envy?

verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est manifesta correptio  quam amor absconditus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֭וֹבָה תּוֹכַ֣חַת מְגֻלָּ֑ה מֵאַהֲבָ֥ה מְסֻתָּֽרֶת׃
verses.king_james: Open rebuke is better than secret love.

verses.latin_vulgate: Meliora sunt vulnera diligentis  quam fraudulenta oscula odientis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֶ֭אֱמָנִים פִּצְעֵ֣י אוֹהֵ֑ב וְ֝נַעְתָּר֗וֹת נְשִׁיק֥וֹת שׂוֹנֵֽא׃
verses.king_james: Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful.

verses.latin_vulgate: Anima saturata calcabit favum,  et anima esuriens etiam amarum pro dulci sumet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֶ֣פֶשׁ שְׂ֭בֵעָה תָּב֣וּס נֹ֑פֶת וְנֶ֥פֶשׁ רְ֝עֵבָ֗ה כׇּל־מַ֥ר מָתֽוֹק׃
verses.king_james: The full soul loatheth an honeycomb; but to the hungry soul every bitter thing is sweet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut avis transmigrans de nido suo,  sic vir qui derelinquit locum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּ֭צִפּוֹר נוֹדֶ֣דֶת מִן־קִנָּ֑הּ כֵּֽן־אִ֝֗ישׁ נוֹדֵ֥ד מִמְּקוֹמֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: As a bird that wandereth from her nest, so is a man that wandereth from his place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unguento et variis odoribus delectatur cor,  et bonis amici consiliis anima dulcoratur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֶׁ֣מֶן וּ֭קְטֹרֶת יְשַׂמַּֽח־לֵ֑ב וּמֶ֥תֶק רֵ֝עֵ֗הוּ מֵעֲצַת־נָֽפֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Ointment and perfume rejoice the heart: so doth the sweetness of a man’s friend by hearty counsel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amicum tuum et amicum patris tui ne dimiseris,  et domum fratris tui ne ingrediaris in die afflictionis tuæ.  Melior est vicinus juxta  quam frater procul.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֵ֥עֲךָ֨ (ורעה) [וְרֵ֪עַ] אָבִ֡יךָ אַֽל־תַּעֲזֹ֗ב וּבֵ֥ית אָחִ֗יךָ אַל־תָּ֭בוֹא בְּי֣וֹם אֵידֶ֑ךָ ט֥וֹב שָׁכֵ֥ן קָ֝ר֗וֹב מֵאָ֥ח רָחֽוֹק׃
verses.king_james: Thine own friend, and thy father’s friend, forsake not; neither go into thy brother’s house in the day of thy calamity: for better is a neighbour that is near than a brother far off.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stude sapientiæ, fili mi, et lætifica cor meum,  ut possis exprobranti respondere sermonem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲכַ֣ם בְּ֭נִי וְשַׂמַּ֣ח לִבִּ֑י וְאָשִׁ֖יבָה חֹרְפִ֣י דָבָֽר׃
verses.king_james: My son, be wise, and make my heart glad, that I may answer him that reproacheth me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Astutus videns malum, absconditus est :  parvuli transeuntes sustinuerunt dispendia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָר֤וּם ׀ רָאָ֣ה רָעָ֣ה נִסְתָּ֑ר פְּ֝תָאיִ֗ם עָבְר֥וּ נֶעֱנָֽשׁוּ׃
verses.king_james: A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself; but the simple pass on, and are punished.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tolle vestimentum ejus qui spopondit pro extraneo,  et pro alienis aufer ei pignus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַח־בִּ֭גְדוֹ כִּי־עָ֣רַב זָ֑ר וּבְעַ֖ד נׇכְרִיָּ֣ה חַבְלֵֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: Take his garment that is surety for a stranger, and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui benedicit proximo suo voce grandi,  de nocte consurgens maledicenti similis erit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְבָ֘רֵ֤ךְ רֵעֵ֨הוּ ׀ בְּק֣וֹל גָּ֭דוֹל בַּבֹּ֣קֶר הַשְׁכֵּ֑ים קְ֝לָלָ֗ה תֵּחָ֥שֶׁב לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: He that blesseth his friend with a loud voice, rising early in the morning, it shall be counted a curse to him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tecta perstillantia in die frigoris  et litigiosa mulier comparantur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דֶּ֣לֶף ט֭וֹרֵד בְּי֣וֹם סַגְרִ֑יר וְאֵ֥שֶׁת (מדונים) [מִ֝דְיָנִ֗ים] נִשְׁתָּוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: A continual dropping in a very rainy day and a contentious woman are alike.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui retinet eam quasi qui ventum teneat,  et oleum dexteræ suæ vocabit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צֹפְנֶ֥יהָ צָפַן־ר֑וּחַ וְשֶׁ֖מֶן יְמִינ֣וֹ יִקְרָֽא׃
verses.king_james: Whosoever hideth her hideth the wind, and the ointment of his right hand, which bewrayeth itself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ferrum ferro exacuitur,  et homo exacuit faciem amici sui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּרְזֶ֣ל בְּבַרְזֶ֣ל יָ֑חַד וְ֝אִ֗ישׁ יַ֣חַד פְּנֵֽי־רֵעֵֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui servat ficum comedet fructus ejus,  et qui custos est domini sui glorificabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֹצֵ֣ר תְּ֭אֵנָה יֹאכַ֣ל פִּרְיָ֑הּ וְשֹׁמֵ֖ר אֲדֹנָ֣יו יְכֻבָּֽד׃
verses.king_james: Whoso keepeth the fig tree shall eat the fruit thereof: so he that waiteth on his master shall be honoured.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo in aquis resplendent vultus prospicientium,  sic corda hominum manifesta sunt prudentibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּ֭מַּיִם הַפָּנִ֣ים לַפָּנִ֑ים כֵּ֤ן לֵֽב־הָ֝אָדָ֗ם לָאָדָֽם׃
verses.king_james: As in water face answereth to face, so the heart of man to man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Infernus et perditio numquam implentur :  similiter et oculi hominum insatiabiles.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁא֣וֹל וַ֭אֲבַדֹּה לֹ֣א תִשְׂבַּ֑עְנָה וְעֵינֵ֥י הָ֝אָדָ֗ם לֹ֣א תִשְׂבַּֽעְנָה׃
verses.king_james: Hell and destruction are never full; so the eyes of man are never satisfied.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo probatur in conflatorio argentum et in fornace aurum,  sic probatur homo ore laudantis.  Cor iniqui inquirit mala,  cor autem rectum inquirit scientiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַצְרֵ֣ף לַ֭כֶּסֶף וְכ֣וּר לַזָּהָ֑ב וְ֝אִ֗ישׁ לְפִ֣י מַהֲלָלֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: As the fining pot for silver, and the furnace for gold; so is a man to his praise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si contuderis stultum in pila  quasi ptisanas feriente desuper pilo,  non auferetur ab eo stultitia ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֥ם־תִּכְתּֽוֹשׁ־אֶת־הָאֱוִ֨יל ׀ בַּ֥מַּכְתֵּ֡שׁ בְּת֣וֹךְ הָ֭רִיפוֹת בַּעֱלִ֑י לֹא־תָס֥וּר מֵ֝עָלָ֗יו אִוַּלְתּֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Though thou shouldest bray a fool in a mortar among wheat with a pestle, yet will not his foolishness depart from him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Diligenter agnosce vultum pecoris tui,  tuosque greges considera :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָדֹ֣עַ תֵּ֭דַע פְּנֵ֣י צֹאנֶ֑ךָ שִׁ֥ית לִ֝בְּךָ֗ לַעֲדָרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Be thou diligent to know the state of thy flocks, and look well to thy herds.

verses.latin_vulgate: non enim habebis jugiter potestatem,  sed corona tribuetur in generationem et generationem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א לְעוֹלָ֣ם חֹ֑סֶן וְאִם־נֵ֝֗זֶר לְד֣וֹר (דור) [וָדֽוֹר]׃
verses.king_james: For riches are not for ever: and doth the crown endure to every generation?

verses.latin_vulgate: Aperta sunt prata, et apparuerunt herbæ virentes,  et collecta sunt fœna de montibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גָּלָ֣ה חָ֭צִיר וְנִרְאָה־דֶ֑שֶׁא וְ֝נֶאֶסְפ֗וּ עִשְּׂב֥וֹת הָרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: The hay appeareth, and the tender grass sheweth itself, and herbs of the mountains are gathered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Agni ad vestimentum tuum,  et hædi ad agri pretium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּבָשִׂ֥ים לִלְבוּשֶׁ֑ךָ וּמְחִ֥יר שָׂ֝דֶ֗ה עַתּוּדִֽים׃
verses.king_james: The lambs are for thy clothing, and the goats are the price of the field.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sufficiat tibi lac caprarum in cibos tuos,  et in necessaria domus tuæ, et ad victum ancillis tuis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדֵ֤י ׀ חֲלֵ֬ב עִזִּ֗ים לְֽ֭לַחְמְךָ לְלֶ֣חֶם בֵּיתֶ֑ךָ וְ֝חַיִּ֗ים לְנַעֲרוֹתֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: And thou shalt have goats’ milk enough for thy food, for the food of thy household, and for the maintenance for thy maidens.
